evals.prompt: I'm working on the OpenHarmony rendering subsystem and I need to understand the two-thread rendering pipeline. Can you explain how RSMainThread and RSUniRenderThread interact, and draw a diagram of the architecture? The code is in the render_pipeline directory.
evals.expected_output: An interactive analysis that (1) asks clarifying questions about scope before diving in, (2) presents the call flow between RSMainThread and RSUniRenderThread for confirmation, (3) generates a markdown documentation file with a mermaid class diagram showing RSRenderNode/RSBaseRenderNode/RSRenderNodeDrawable hierarchy and a sequence diagram showing the Vsync → Prepare → Sync → Render flow with code provenance (file paths and line numbers).

assertions.name: asks-clarifying-questions
assertions.expected: The response should include clarifying questions about scope, depth, or specific aspects the user wants to understand before proceeding to analysis

assertions.name: presents-call-flow-for-confirmation
assertions.expected: The response should present a call flow or architecture overview and ask for confirmation before generating full documentation

assertions.name: diagrams-have-code-provenance
assertions.expected: Documentation should reference specific file paths and line numbers (e.g., render_thread.cpp:32 or render_node.h:15) to anchor diagram elements to actual code

assertions.name: captures-async-error-paths
assertions.expected: The analysis should mention or trace the error/async paths: the condition_variable wait pattern, invalid fence handling, or the null drawable check in OnDraw

assertions.name: produces-markdown-doc-file
assertions.expected: Should create a markdown documentation file with sections like Overview, Architecture, Flow Description, Key Components

evals.prompt: Help me understand the SyncFence caching system in the graphics module. I need documentation with class diagrams showing how SyncFence, FenceCache, SyncFenceManager, and RenderFencePool relate to each other. The source files are in the fence_cache directory.
evals.expected_output: An interactive analysis that (1) asks about depth and focus areas, (2) presents the fence lifecycle flow for confirmation, (3) generates documentation with a mermaid class diagram showing the ownership/dependency relationships between the four classes, and a sequence diagram showing the GetOrCreateFence → CreateFenceFromSemaphore → FenceCache Insert flow.

assertions.name: asks-clarifying-questions
assertions.expected: The response should ask clarifying questions about scope or depth before proceeding

assertions.name: presents-call-flow-for-confirmation
assertions.expected: The response should present a call flow and ask for confirmation

assertions.name: diagrams-have-code-provenance
assertions.expected: Documentation should reference specific file paths and line numbers to anchor diagram elements

assertions.name: captures-second-pass-error-paths
assertions.expected: Should mention error paths: null semaphore callback, invalid fd (< 0), or expired cache entry cleanup logic

assertions.name: produces-markdown-doc-file
assertions.expected: Should create a markdown documentation file with structured sections

evals.prompt: Explain the DisplayConfig and display mode calculation functions in the display_config directory. I just need to understand what these functions do.
evals.expected_output: An interactive analysis that correctly identifies this as a simple data-structure + linear-function module and either (1) skips diagrams entirely, or (2) uses a table/text format instead. Should NOT force a class diagram (fewer than 3 meaningful types) or sequence diagram (purely linear flow).

assertions.name: asks-clarifying-questions
assertions.expected: The response should ask clarifying questions about scope or what the user needs

assertions.name: does-not-force-class-diagram
assertions.note: The module has fewer than 3 meaningful classes — just 2 structs and 3 standalone functions. A class diagram should be skipped per the skill's guidance

assertions.name: does-not-force-sequence-diagram
assertions.note: The flow is purely linear with no branching or multi-component interaction — a sequence diagram should be skipped per the skill's guidance

assertions.name: uses-table-or-text-for-config
assertions.expected: Should use a table or text description to document the data structures and functions, since this is a config/schema module

assertions.name: explains-functions-clearly
assertions.expected: Should explain what GetDefaultDisplayMode, CalculateDensityDpi, and GetScaleFactorForDpi do
